definitions.definition: Moving around; astir.

definitions.definition: Indicating unproductive or unstructured activity.

definitions.definition: Nearly; approximately; with close correspondence in quality, manner, degree, quantity, or time; almost.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity.
To a reversed order; half round; facing in the opposite direction; from a contrary point of view.

definitions.definition: In succession; one after another; in the course of events.
In circuit; circularly; by a circuitous way; around the outside; in circumference.

definitions.definition: In a circle around; all round; on every side of; on the outside of.

definitions.definition: Over or upon different parts of; through or over in various directions; here and there in; to and fro in; throughout.

definitions.definition: On one's person; nearby the person.

definitions.definition: In or near, as in mental faculties or in the possession of; under the control of; at one's command; in one's makeup.

definitions.definition: Heaven.
Something, especially a person's name in legal documents, that appears higher on the same page or on a preceding page.
Higher authority.

Of heaven; heavenly.

Directly overhead; vertically on top of.
Higher in the same page; earlier in the order as far as writing products go.

definitions.definition: In a higher place; upstairs; farther upstream.
Higher in rank, power, or position.

More in number.
Above zero; above freezing.

definitions.definition: In or to a higher place; higher than; on or over the upper surface.

definitions.definition: Higher in rank, status, or position.

Any variety of bread.
Money.
Food; sustenance; support of life, in general.

definitions.definition: The separation between lines, paragraphs or pages of a written text.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.

A department of a newspaper tasked with covering a particular geographical region or aspect of the news.
definitions.example: city desk

definitions.definition: To shut up, as in a desk; to treasure.
To equip with a desk or desks.
Something small enough to escape casual notice.
definitions.example: Note this fine detail in the lower left corner.

definitions.definition: A profusion of details.
definitions.example: This etching is full of fine detail.

definitions.definition: The small things that can escape casual notice.
Something considered trivial enough to ignore.
definitions.example: I don't concern myself with the details of accounting.

definitions.definition: A person's name, address and other personal information.
definitions.example: The arresting officer asked the suspect for his details.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) A temporary unit or assignment.
An individual feature, fact, or other item, considered separately from the whole of which it is a part.
A narrative which relates minute points; an account which dwells on particulars.
A selected portion of a painting
To explain in detail.
definitions.example: I'll detail the exact procedure to you later.

definitions.definition: To clean carefully (particularly of road vehicles) (always pronounced. /ˈdiːteɪl/)
definitions.example: We need to have the minivan detailed.

definitions.definition: To assign to a particular task
A conversation or other form of discourse between two or more individuals.
definitions.example: Bill and Melinda maintained a dialogue via email over the course of their long-distance relationship.

definitions.definition: (authorship) In a dramatic or literary presentation, the verbal parts of the script or text; the verbalizations of the actors or characters.
definitions.example: The movie had great special effects, but the dialogue was lackluster.

definitions.definition: A literary form, where the presentation resembles a conversation.
definitions.example: A literary historian, she specialized in the dialogues of ancient Greek philosophers.

definitions.definition: A dialogue box.
definitions.example: Once the My Computer dialogue opens, select Local Disk (C:), then right click and scroll down.

definitions.definition: To discuss or negotiate so that all parties can reach an understanding.
definitions.example: Pearson wanted to dialogue with his overseas counterparts about the new reporting requirements.

definitions.definition: To put into dialogue form.
To take part in a dialogue; to dialogize.

A reference work with a list of words from one or more languages, normally ordered alphabetically, explaining each word's meaning, and sometimes containing information on its etymology, pronunciation, usage, translations, and other data.
(preceded by the) A synchronic dictionary of a standardised language held to only contain words that are properly part of the language.
(by extension) Any work that has a list of material organized alphabetically; e.g., biographical dictionary, encyclopedic dictionary.
An associative array, a data structure where each value is referenced by a particular key, analogous to words and definitions in a physical dictionary.
To look up in a dictionary.
To add to a dictionary.
To compile a dictionary.
(crosswords, often in combination) A word that runs horizontally in the completed puzzle grid or its associated clue.
definitions.example: I solved all of the acrosses, but then got stuck on 3 down.

definitions.definition: From one side to the other.
definitions.example: she helped the blind man across;  the river is half a mile across

definitions.definition: On the other side.
definitions.example: If we sail off at noon, when will we be across?

definitions.definition: In a particular direction.
definitions.example: He leaned across for a book.

definitions.definition: (crosswords) Horizontally.
definitions.example: I got stuck on 4 across.

definitions.definition: To, toward or from the far side of (something that lies between two points of interest).
definitions.example: Fortunately, there was a bridge across the river.

definitions.definition: On the opposite side of (something that lies between two points of interest).
definitions.example: That store is across the street.

definitions.definition: Across from: on the opposite side, relative to something that lies between, from (a point of interest).
From one side to the other within (a space being traversed).
definitions.example: Could you slide that across the table to me, please?

definitions.definition: At or near the far end of (a space).
Spanning.
definitions.example: This poetry speaks across the centuries.

definitions.definition: Throughout.
definitions.example: All across the country, voters were communicating their representatives.

definitions.definition: So as to intersect or pass through or over at an angle.
definitions.example: Lay the top stick across the bottom one.

definitions.definition: In possession of full, up-to-date information about; abreast of.
(with a possessive pronoun) Everything that one is capable of.
definitions.example: She gave her all, and collapsed at the finish line.

definitions.definition: The totality of one's possessions.
All gone; dead.
definitions.example: The butter is all.

definitions.definition: (degree) Intensifier.
definitions.example: It suddenly went all quiet.

definitions.definition: Entirely; completely; totally.
Apiece; each.
definitions.example: The score was 30 all when the rain delay started.

definitions.definition: (degree) so much.
definitions.example: Don't want to go? All the better since I lost the tickets.

definitions.definition: Even; just.
Everything.
definitions.example: She knows all and sees all.

definitions.definition: Everyone.
definitions.example: A good time was had by all.

definitions.definition: The only thing(s).
definitions.example: All that was left was a small pile of ash.

definitions.definition: Used after who, what, where, how and similar words, either without changing their meaning, or indicating that one expects that they cover more than one element, e.g. that "who all attended" is more than one person. (Some dialects only allow this to follow some words and not others.)
Clipping of y’all. Used only as a vocative.
Although.
To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death.
To (stop living and) undergo (a specified death).
definitions.example: He died a hero's death.

definitions.definition: To yearn intensely.
definitions.example: I'm dying for a packet of crisps.

definitions.definition: To be or become hated or utterly ignored or cut off, as if dead.
definitions.example: The day our sister eloped, she died to our mother.

definitions.definition: To become spiritually dead; to lose hope.
definitions.example: He died a little inside each time she refused to speak to him.

definitions.definition: To be mortified or shocked by a situation.
definitions.example: If anyone sees me wearing this ridiculous outfit, I'll die.

definitions.definition: To be so overcome with emotion or laughter as to be incapacitated.
definitions.example: When I found out my two favorite musicians would be recording an album together, I literally planned my own funeral arrangements and died.

definitions.definition: (of a machine) To stop working, to break down.
definitions.example: My car died in the middle of the freeway this morning.

definitions.definition: (of a computer program) To abort, to terminate (as an error condition).
To perish; to cease to exist; to become lost or extinct.
To sink; to faint; to pine; to languish, with weakness, discouragement, love, etc.
(often with "to") To become indifferent; to cease to be subject.
definitions.example: to die to pleasure or to sin

definitions.definition: To disappear gradually in another surface, as where mouldings are lost in a sloped or curved face.
To become vapid, flat, or spiritless, as liquor.
(of a stand-up comedian or a joke) To fail to evoke laughter from the audience.
definitions.example: Then there was that time I died onstage in Montreal...

definitions.definition: The food and beverage a person or animal consumes.
definitions.example: The diet of the Giant Panda consists mainly of bamboo.

definitions.definition: A controlled regimen of food and drink, as to gain or lose weight or otherwise influence health.
(by extension) Any habitual intake or consumption.
definitions.example: He's been reading a steady diet of nonfiction for the last several years.

definitions.definition: (of a food or beverage) Containing less fat, salt, sugar, or calories than normal, or claimed to have such.
definitions.example: diet soda

definitions.definition: Having certain traits subtracted.
definitions.example: You folks reduce it to the bible only as being authoritative, impoverishing the faith. "Christianity Lite", diet Christianity for those who can't handle the Whole Meal.

definitions.definition: The quality of being different.
definitions.example: You need to learn to be more tolerant of difference.

definitions.definition: A characteristic of something that makes it different from something else.
definitions.example: There are three differences between these two pictures.

definitions.definition: A disagreement or argument.
definitions.example: We have our little differences, but we are firm friends.

definitions.definition: Significant change in or effect on a situation or state.
definitions.example: It just won't make much difference to me.

definitions.definition: The result of a subtraction; sometimes the absolute value of this result.
definitions.example: The difference between 3 and 21 is 18.

definitions.definition: Choice; preference.
An addition to a coat of arms to distinguish two people's bearings which would otherwise be the same. See augmentation and cadency.
The quality or attribute which is added to those of the genus to constitute a species; a differentia.
(logic circuits) A Boolean operation which is TRUE when the two input variables are different but is otherwise FALSE; the XOR operation (\scriptstyle A \overline B + \overline A B).
(relational algebra) the set of elements that are in one set but not another (\scriptstyle A \overline B).
To distinguish or differentiate.
The different ideal.
Not the same; exhibiting a difference.
Various, assorted, diverse.
Distinct, separate; used for emphasis after numbers and other determiners of quantity.
definitions.example: Several different scientists all reached this conclusion at about the same time.

definitions.definition: Unlike most others; unusual.
Differently.
To make difficult; to impede; to perplex.
Hard, not easy, requiring much effort.
definitions.example: However, the difficult weather conditions will ensure Yunnan has plenty of freshwater.

definitions.definition: (often of a person, or a horse, etc) Hard to manage, uncooperative, troublesome.
definitions.example: Stop being difficult and eat your broccoli—you know it's good for you.

definitions.definition: Unable or unwilling.
A midday meal (in a context in which the evening meal is called supper or tea).
The main meal of the day, often eaten in the evening.
An evening meal.
A meal given to an animal.
definitions.example: Give the dog its dinner.

definitions.definition: A formal meal for many people eaten for a special occasion.
The food provided or consumed at any such meal.
To eat a dinner.
To provide (someone) with a dinner.
To make (something) dirty.
To stain or tarnish (somebody) with dishonor.
To debase by distorting the real nature of (something).
To become soiled.
Unclean; covered with or containing unpleasant substances such as dirt or grime.
definitions.example: Despite a walk in the rain, my shoes weren't too dirty.

definitions.definition: That makes one unclean; corrupting, infecting.
definitions.example: Don't put that in your mouth, dear. It's dirty.

definitions.definition: Morally unclean; obscene or indecent, especially sexually.
definitions.example: At the reception, Uncle Nick got drunk and told dirty jokes to the bridesmaids.

definitions.definition: Dishonourable; violating accepted standards or rules.
definitions.example: He might have scored, but it was a dirty trick that won him the penalty.

definitions.definition: Corrupt, illegal, or improper.
definitions.example: I won't accept your dirty money!

definitions.definition: Out of tune.
definitions.example: You need to tune that guitar: the G string sounds dirty.

definitions.definition: Of color, discolored by impurities.
definitions.example: The old flag was a dirty white.

definitions.definition: Containing data needing to be written back to memory or disk.
definitions.example: Occasionally it reads the sector into a dirty buffer, which means it needs to sync the dirty buffer first.

definitions.definition: Carrying illegal drugs among one's possessions or inside of one's bloodstream.
definitions.example: None of y'all get into my car if you're dirty.

definitions.definition: Used as an intensifier, especially in conjunction with "great".
definitions.example: He lives in a dirty great mansion.

definitions.definition: Sleety; gusty; stormy.
definitions.example: dirty weather

definitions.definition: Of an alcoholic beverage, especially a cocktail or mixed drink: served with the juice of olives.
definitions.example: dirty martini

definitions.definition: Of food, indulgent in an unhealthy way.
definitions.example: The waiter served dirty burgers to the customers.

definitions.definition: In a dirty manner.
definitions.example: to play dirty

definitions.definition: To converse or debate concerning a particular topic.
definitions.example: I don't wish to discuss this further. Let's talk about something else.

definitions.definition: To communicate, tell, or disclose (information, a message, etc.).
To break to pieces; to shatter.
To deal with, in eating or drinking; consume.
To examine or search thoroughly; to exhaust a remedy against, as against a principal debtor before proceeding against the surety.
To drive away, disperse, shake off; said especially of tumors.
A vessel such as a plate for holding or serving food, often flat with a depressed region in the middle.
The contents of such a vessel.
definitions.example: a dish of stew

definitions.definition: (metonym) A specific type of prepared food.
definitions.example: a vegetable dish

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Tableware (including cutlery, etc, as well as crockery) that is to be or is being washed after being used to prepare, serve and eat a meal.
definitions.example: It's your turn to wash the dishes.

definitions.definition: A type of antenna with a similar shape to a plate or bowl.
definitions.example: radar dish

definitions.definition: A sexually attractive person.
The state of being concave, like a dish, or the degree of such concavity.
definitions.example: the dish of a wheel

definitions.definition: A hollow place, as in a field.
A trough in which ore is measured.
That portion of the produce of a mine which is paid to the land owner or proprietor.
Gossip
To put in a dish or dishes; serve, usually food.
definitions.example: The restaurant dished up a delicious Italian brunch.

definitions.definition: To gossip; to relay information about the personal situation of another.
To make concave, or depress in the middle, like a dish.
definitions.example: to dish a wheel by inclining the spokes

definitions.definition: To frustrate; to beat; to outwit or defeat.
A party, celebration, social function.
definitions.example: We’re having a bit of a do on Saturday to celebrate my birthday.

definitions.definition: A hairdo.
definitions.example: Nice do!

definitions.definition: Something that can or should be done (usually in the phrase dos and don'ts).
A deed; an act.
Ado; bustle; stir; to-do; A period of confusion or argument.
A cheat; a swindler.
An act of swindling; a fraud or deception.
(auxiliary) A syntactic marker.
To perform; to execute.
definitions.example: All you ever do is surf the Internet. What will you do this afternoon?

definitions.definition: To cause, make (someone) (do something).
To suffice.
definitions.example: it’s not the best broom, but it will have to do;  this will do me, thanks.

definitions.definition: To be reasonable or acceptable.
definitions.example: It simply will not do to have dozens of children running around such a quiet event.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To have (as an effect).
definitions.example: The fresh air did him some good.

definitions.definition: To fare, perform (well or poorly).
definitions.example: Our relationship isn't doing very well;  how do you do?

definitions.definition: (chiefly in questions) To have as one's job.
definitions.example: What does Bob do? — He's a plumber.

definitions.definition: To perform the tasks or actions associated with (something).
definitions.example: "Don't forget to do your report" means something quite different depending on whether you're a student or a programmer.

definitions.definition: To cook.
definitions.example: I'll just do some eggs.

definitions.definition: To travel in, to tour, to make a circuit of.
definitions.example: Let’s do New York also.

definitions.definition: To treat in a certain way.
To work for or on, by way of caring for, looking after, preparing, cleaning, keeping in order, etc.
To act or behave in a certain manner; to conduct oneself.
To spend (time) in jail. (See also do time)
definitions.example: I did five years for armed robbery.

definitions.definition: To impersonate or depict.
definitions.example: They really laughed when he did Clinton, with a perfect accent and a leer.

definitions.definition: (with 'a' and the name of a person, place, event, etc.) To copy or emulate the actions or behaviour that is associated with the person or thing mentioned.
definitions.example: He did a Henry VIII and got married six times.

definitions.definition: To kill.
To deal with for good and all; to finish up; to undo; to ruin; to do for.
To punish for a misdemeanor.
definitions.example: He got done for speeding.

definitions.definition: To have sex with. (See also do it)
To cheat or swindle.
definitions.example: That guy just did me out of two hundred bucks!

definitions.definition: To convert into a certain form; especially, to translate.
definitions.example: the novel has just been done into English;  I'm going to do this play into a movie

definitions.definition: To finish.
To work as a domestic servant (with for).
(auxiliary) Used to form the present progressive of verbs.
To cash or to advance money for, as a bill or note.
(ditransitive) To make or provide.
definitions.example: Could you do me a burger with mayonnaise instead of ketchup?

definitions.definition: To injure (one's own body part).
To take drugs.
definitions.example: I do cocaine.

definitions.definition: (in the form be doing [somewhere]) To exist with a purpose or for a reason.
definitions.example: What's that car doing in our swimming pool?

definitions.definition: (focus) In addition; besides; as well; further; too.
definitions.example: They had porridge for breakfast, and also toast.

definitions.definition: To the same degree or extent; so, as.

A physician; a member of the medical profession; one who is trained and licensed to heal the sick or injured. The final examination and qualification may award a doctor degree in which case the post-nominal letters are D.O., DPM, M.D., DMD, DDS, in the US or MBBS in the UK.
definitions.example: If you still feel unwell tomorrow, see your doctor.

definitions.definition: A person who has attained a doctorate, such as a Ph.D. or Th.D. or one of many other terminal degrees conferred by a college or university.
A veterinarian; a medical practitioner who treats non-human animals.
A nickname for a person who has special knowledge or talents to manipulate or arrange transactions.
A teacher; one skilled in a profession or a branch of knowledge; a learned man.
Any mechanical contrivance intended to remedy a difficulty or serve some purpose in an exigency.
definitions.example: the doctor of a calico-printing machine, which is a knife to remove superfluous colouring matter

definitions.definition: A fish, the friar skate.
To act as a medical doctor to.
definitions.example: Her children doctored her back to health.

definitions.definition: To act as a medical doctor.
To make (someone) into an (academic) doctor; to confer a doctorate upon.
To physically alter (medically or surgically) a living being in order to change growth or behavior.
definitions.example: They doctored their apple trees by vigorous pruning, and now the dwarfed trees are easier to pick.

definitions.definition: To genetically alter an extant species.
definitions.example: Mendel's discoveries showed how the evolution of a species may be doctored.

definitions.definition: To alter or make obscure, as with the intention to deceive, especially a document.
To take medicine.
A mammal, Canis familiaris or Canis lupus familiaris, that has been domesticated for thousands of years, of highly variable appearance due to human breeding.
definitions.example: The dog barked all night long.

definitions.definition: Any member of the Family Canidae, including domestic dogs, wolves, coyotes, jackals, foxes, and their relatives (extant and extinct); canid.
A male dog, wolf or fox, as opposed to a bitch or vixen.
A dull, unattractive girl or woman.
definitions.example: She’s a real dog.

definitions.definition: A man (derived from definition 2).
definitions.example: He's a silly dog.

definitions.definition: A coward.
definitions.example: Come back and fight, you dogs!

definitions.definition: Someone who is morally reprehensible.
definitions.example: You dirty dog.

definitions.definition: A sexually aggressive man (cf. horny).
Any of various mechanical devices for holding, gripping, or fastening something, particularly with a tooth-like projection.
A click or pallet adapted to engage the teeth of a ratchet-wheel, to restrain the back action; a click or pawl. (See also: ratchet, windlass)
A metal support for logs in a fireplace.
definitions.example: The dogs were too hot to touch.

definitions.definition: The eighteenth Lenormand card.
A hot dog.
Underdog.
(almost always in the plural) Foot.
(from "dog and bone") Phone or mobile phone.
definitions.example: My dog is dead.

definitions.definition: One of the cones used to divide up a racetrack when training horses.
A flop; a film that performs poorly at the box office.
To pursue with the intent to catch.
To follow in an annoying or harassing way.
definitions.example: The woman cursed him so that trouble would dog his every step.

definitions.definition: To fasten a hatch securely.
definitions.example: It is very important to dog down these hatches...

definitions.definition: To watch, or participate, in sexual activity in a public place.
definitions.example: I admit that I like to dog at my local country park.

definitions.definition: To intentionally restrict one's productivity as employee; to work at the slowest rate that goes unpunished.
definitions.example: A surprise inspection of the night shift found that some workers were dogging it.

definitions.definition: To criticize.
To divide (a watch) with a comrade.
Meat from a dog eaten as food.
definitions.example: We visited South Korea this time around, where we ate dog meat for the first time.

definitions.definition: Meat prepared to be given to a dog as food.
An insult intended to assert hyperbolically that another person has value only as a corpse to be fed to a dog.
definitions.example: Did you just step on my blue suede shoes? You're dog meat now!

definitions.definition: Official designation for currency in some parts of the world, including Canada, the United States, Australia, New Zealand, Hong Kong, and elsewhere. Its symbol is $.
(by extension) Money generally.
A quarter of a pound or one crown, historically minted as a coin of approximately the same size and composition as a then-contemporary dollar coin of the United States, and worth slightly more.
Imported from the United States, and paid for in U.S. dollars. (Note: distinguish "dollar wheat", North American farmers' slogan, meaning a market price of one dollar per bushel.)

A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge. Doors are frequently made of wood or metal. May have a handle to help open and close, a latch to hold the door closed and a lock that ensures the door cannot be opened without the key.
definitions.example: I knocked on the vice president's door

definitions.definition: Any flap, etc. that opens like a door.
definitions.example: the 24 doors in an Advent calendar

definitions.definition: (immigration) An entry point.
A means of approach or access.
definitions.example: Learning is the door to wisdom.

definitions.definition: A barrier.
definitions.example: Keep a door on your anger.

definitions.definition: A software mechanism by which a user can interact with a program running remotely on a bulletin board system. See BBS door.
To cause a collision by opening the door of a vehicle in front of an oncoming cyclist or pedestrian.
A negative aspect; a downer.
definitions.example: I love almost everything about my job. The only down is that I can't take Saturdays off.

definitions.definition: A grudge (on someone).
An act of swallowing an entire drink at once.
A single play, from the time the ball is snapped (the start) to the time the whistle is blown (the end) when the ball is down, or is downed.
definitions.example: I bet after the third down, the kicker will replace the quarterback on the field.

definitions.definition: (crosswords) A clue whose solution runs vertically in the grid.
definitions.example: I haven't solved 12 or 13 across, but I've got most of the downs.

definitions.definition: A downstairs room of a two-story house.
definitions.example: She lives in a two-up two-down.

definitions.definition: Down payment.
To knock (someone or something) down; to cause to come down, to fell.
definitions.example: A single rifle shot downed the mighty beast.

definitions.definition: To lower; to put (something) down.
definitions.example: The bell rang for lunch, and the workers downed their tools.

definitions.definition: To defeat; to overpower.
To disparage, to put down.
To go or come down; to descend.
To drink or swallow, especially without stopping before the vessel containing the liquid is empty.
definitions.example: He downed an ale and ordered another.

definitions.definition: To render (the ball) dead, typically by touching the ground while in possession.
definitions.example: He downed it at the seven-yard line.

definitions.definition: (pocket billiards) To sink (a ball) into a hole or pocket.
definitions.example: He downed two balls on the break.

definitions.definition: Sad, unhappy, depressed, feeling low.
definitions.example: Mary seems very down since she split up with her boyfriend.

definitions.definition: (normally in the combination 'down with') Sick or ill.
definitions.example: He is down with the flu.

definitions.definition: At a lower level than before.
definitions.example: Prices are down.

definitions.definition: Having a lower score than an opponent.
definitions.example: At 5–1 down, she produced a great comeback to win the set on a tiebreak.

definitions.definition: (following the noun modified) Out.
definitions.example: Two down and one to go in the bottom of the ninth.

definitions.definition: (with "on") Negative about, hostile to.
definitions.example: Ever since Nixon, I've been down on Republicans.

definitions.definition: Comfortable with, accepting of, approachable.
definitions.example: Are you down to hang out at the mall, Jamal?

definitions.definition: Inoperable; out of order; out of service.
definitions.example: The system is down.

definitions.definition: Finished (of a task); defeated or dealt with (of an opponent or obstacle); elapsed (of time). Often coupled with to go (remaining).
definitions.example: Ten minutes down and nothing's happened yet.

definitions.definition: (police, of a person) Wounded and unable to move normally, or killed.
definitions.example: There are three soldiers down and one walking wounded.

definitions.definition: (of an aircraft) Mechanically failed, collided, shot down, or otherwise suddenly unable to fly.
definitions.example: We have a chopper down near the river.

definitions.definition: Thoroughly practiced, learned or memorised; mastered. (Compare down pat.)
definitions.example: It's two weeks until opening night and our lines are still not down yet.

definitions.definition: Downright; absolute; positive.
Accepted or respected, especially in the black or thug community.
definitions.example: What you mean, 'No'? Man, I thought you was down.

definitions.definition: Facing downwards.
From a higher position to a lower one; downwards.
definitions.example: The cat jumped down from the table.

definitions.definition: At a lower or further place or position along a set path.
definitions.example: His place is farther down the road.

definitions.definition: South (as south is at the bottom of typical maps).
definitions.example: I went down to Miami for a conference.

definitions.definition: Away from the city (regardless of direction).
definitions.example: He went down to Cavan.

definitions.definition: At or towards any place that is visualised as 'down' by virtue of local features or local convention, or arbitrarily, irrespective of direction or elevation change.
definitions.example: She lives down by the park.

definitions.definition: Towards the opponent's side (in ball-sports).
Into a state of non-operation.
definitions.example: The computer has been shut down.

definitions.definition: To a subordinate or less prestigious position or rank.
definitions.example: After the incident, Kelly went down to Second Lieutenant.

definitions.definition: In the direction leading away from the principal terminus, away from milepost zero.
(sentence substitute) Get down.
definitions.example: Down, boy! (such as to direct a dog to stand on four legs from two, or to sit from standing on four legs.)

definitions.definition: (academia) Away from Oxford or Cambridge.
definitions.example: He's gone back down to Newcastle for Christmas.

definitions.definition: From a remoter or higher antiquity.
definitions.example: These traditions have been handed down over generations.

definitions.definition: So as to lessen quantity, level or intensity.
definitions.example: Please turn the music down!

definitions.definition: So as to reduce size, weight or volume.
definitions.example: Boil the mixture down to a syrupy consistency.

definitions.definition: From less to greater detail.
definitions.example: This spreadsheet lets you drill down to daily or even hourly sales figures.

definitions.definition: So as to secure or compress something to the floor, ground, or other (usually horizontal) surface.
definitions.example: We need to nail down this carpet so people don't keep tripping over it.

definitions.definition: (intensifier) Used with verbs to add emphasis to the action of the verb.
definitions.example: They tamped (down) the asphalt to get a better bond.

definitions.definition: Used with verbs to indicate that the action of the verb was carried to some state of completion, rather than being of indefinite duration.

definitions.definition: Having great enthusiasm.

definitions.definition: Having an erection; erect.
Sexually aroused.
To stir the emotions of.

definitions.definition: To arouse or bring out (e.g. feelings); to stimulate.

definitions.definition: To cause an electron to move to a higher than normal state; to promote an electron to an outer level.

definitions.definition: To set in action; to cause to act, move, or make exertion; to give employment to.

To unfold or make visible.
To make something plain or intelligible.
Something additional, such as an item above and beyond the ordinary school curriculum, or added to the usual charge on a bill.

definitions.definition: "Hello!" or an equivalent greeting.

definitions.definition: The form of she used after a preposition or as the object of a verb; that woman, that ship, etc.

definitions.definition: A friendly, informal, casual greeting said when meeting someone.

definitions.definition: An exclamation to call attention.

A communication, or what is communicated; any concept or information conveyed.

definitions.definition: An underlying theme or conclusion to be drawn from something.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) An errand.

To send a message to; to transmit a message to, e.g. as text via a cell phone.

definitions.definition: To send (something) as a message; usually refers to electronic messaging.

definitions.definition: To send a message or messages; to be capable of sending messages.

definitions.definition: To bear as a message.
(always meter) A device that measures things.

(always meter) One who metes or measures.

definitions.definition: (elsewhere metre) The base unit of length in the International System of Units (SI), conceived of as 1/10000000 of the distance from the North Pole to the Equator, and now defined as the distance light will travel in a vacuum in 1/299792458 second.
(elsewhere metre) An increment of music; the overall rhythm; particularly, the number of beats in a measure.
(elsewhere metre) The rhythm pattern in a poem.
(elsewhere metre) A line above or below a hanging net, to which the net is attached in order to strengthen it.
A poem.
To measure with a metering device.
To imprint a postage mark with a postage meter.
To regulate the flow of or to deliver in regulated amounts (usually of fluids but sometimes of other things such as anticipation or breath).
The middle of the night: the sixth temporal hour, equidistant between sunset and sunrise.
12 o'clock at night exactly.
Utterly dark or black.
The international mile: a unit of length precisely equal to 1.609344 kilometers established by treaty among Anglophone nations in 1959, divided into 5,280 feet or 1,760 yards.
Any of several customary units of length derived from the 1593 English statute mile of 8 furlongs, equivalent to 5,280 feet or 1,760 yards of various precise values.
Any of many customary units of length derived from the Roman mile (mille passus) of 8 stades or 5,000 Roman feet.
The Scandinavian mile: a unit of length precisely equal to 10 kilometers defined in 1889.
Any of many customary units of length from other measurement systems of roughly similar values, as the Chinese (里) or Arabic mile (al-mīl).
(travel) An airline mile in a frequent flier program.
Any similarly large distance.

definitions.definition: A race of 1 mile's length; a race of around 1 mile's length (usually 1500 or 1600 meters)

definitions.definition: One mile per hour, as a measure of speed.

definitions.definition: A white liquid produced by the mammary glands of female mammals to nourish their young. From certain animals, especially cows, it is also called dairy milk and is a common food for humans as a beverage or used to produce various dairy products such as butter, cheese, and yogurt.
A white (or whitish) liquid obtained from a vegetable source such as almonds, coconuts, oats, rice, and/or soy beans. Also called non-dairy milk.
An individual serving of milk.

definitions.definition: An individual portion of milk, such as found in a creamer, for tea and coffee.

definitions.definition: The ripe, undischarged spat of an oyster.
Semen.
(long and short scales) The cardinal number 1,000,000: 106; a thousand thousand.
An unspecified very large number.

definitions.definition: A unit of time equal to sixty seconds (one-sixtieth of an hour).

definitions.definition: A short but unspecified time period.

definitions.definition: A unit of angle equal to one-sixtieth of a degree.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural, minutes) A (usually formal) written record of a meeting or a part of a meeting.

definitions.definition: A point in time; a moment.
A nautical or a geographic mile.
An old coin, a half farthing.
A very small part of anything, or anything very small; a jot; a whit.
A fixed part of a module.
A while or a long unspecified period of time

definitions.definition: Of an event, to write in a memo or the minutes of a meeting.

definitions.definition: To set down a short sketch or note of; to jot down; to make a minute or a brief summary of.
A failure to hit.
A failure to obtain or accomplish.
An act of avoidance (used with the verb give).

definitions.definition: The situation where an item is not found in a cache and therefore needs to be explicitly loaded.
To fail to hit.

definitions.definition: To fail to achieve or attain.

definitions.definition: To avoid; to escape.

definitions.definition: To be late for something (a means of transportation, a deadline, etc.).

definitions.definition: To fail to score (a goal).
To go wrong; to err.
To be absent, deficient, or wanting.
An error; a blunder.
A pitch which was intended to be pitched in a hard-to-hit location, but instead ends up in an easy-to-hit place.

definitions.definition: To misunderstand (someone).
To commit an unintentional error; to do or think something wrong.
To take or choose wrongly.
To depart; to go to another place.

definitions.definition: Not here, gone, absent, unavailable, traveling; on vacation.

definitions.definition: At a specified distance in space, time, or figuratively.

definitions.definition: Not on one's home territory.

definitions.definition: (following the noun modified) Out.

definitions.definition: From a place, hence.

definitions.definition: Aside; off; in another direction.

definitions.definition: At a stated distance in time or space.

definitions.definition: In or to something's usual or proper storage place.

definitions.definition: Without restraint.

definitions.definition: Come on!; go on!

definitions.definition: A person, usually an attractive female, hired to show items or goods to the public, such as items given away as prizes on a TV game show.

Someone who lives in modern times.
Pertaining to a current or recent time and style; not ancient.

(familiar) mother.

definitions.definition: A period of 30 days, 31 days, or some alternation thereof.

definitions.definition: Superlative of many

Wine made from grapes that grow on a mountain.

To a great extent.

Note that in an ordinary context, when talking about a bag of apples, one does not care about identifying the individual apples, although one might be interested in distinguishing apples by species, for example, letting 'r' denote 'red apple' and 'g' denote 'green apple', then a bag of three red apples and two green apples could be denoted as {r,r,r,g,g}.

£1000, a grand.

(modifying a numeral, one to twelve) In conjunction with a numeral, indicates the time within a twelve-hour period (midnight to noon or noon to midnight), specifically the time when the hour hand of a clock points precisely to the symbol or marking corresponding to the designated numeral.

definitions.definition: In conjunction with a term representing an action or event that occurs daily, indicates the time that said action or event occurs, or first occurs.

(with a numeral, one to twelve) In conjunction with a numeral, indicates the direction, relative to the speaker or a vehicle, especially an aircraft, corresponding to the direction the hour hand is pointing at the time corresponding to the numeral, with twelve representing directly ahead and three being to the right.
Expressing distance or motion.
Expressing separation.
Expressing origin.
Expressing agency.
Expressing composition, substance.
Introducing subject matter.
Having partitive effect.
Expressing possession.
Forming the "objective genitive".
Expressing qualities or characteristics.
Expressing a point in time.

definitions.definition: Generated by.

A particularly special or compatible person or thing.

definitions.definition: To cause to become one; to gather into a single whole; to unite.

definitions.definition: In agreement.

definitions.definition: The same.

definitions.definition: Being a preeminent example.

definitions.definition: Being an unknown person with the specified name; see also "a certain".

definitions.definition: The first positive number in the set of natural numbers.

(impersonal pronoun, indefinite) One thing (among a group of others); one member of a group.

definitions.definition: (indefinite personal pronoun) Any person (applying to people in general).

definitions.definition: (pronoun) Any person, entity or thing.

The bulb of such a plant.
The genus as a whole.
A ball.
A person from Bermuda or of Bermudian descent.
To bring (a system, etc.) online; to promote to an active or running state.
Of a system: connected (generally electrically) to a larger network.
Available over, or delivered from, the Internet.

definitions.definition: Connected to the Internet.

definitions.definition: Available on a computer system, even if not networked.

definitions.definition: While online; while in a running or active state, or connected to the Internet.

definitions.definition: An only child.
Alone in a category.

definitions.definition: Singularly superior; the best.
Without sibling; without a sibling of the same gender.

Without others or anything further; exclusively.

definitions.definition: No more than; just.

definitions.definition: As recently as.

definitions.definition: Used to express surprise or consternation at an action.

definitions.definition: Above all others; particularly.

definitions.definition: Actively conducting or prepared to conduct business.

definitions.definition: Receptive.

definitions.definition: Public

definitions.definition: Candid, ingenuous, not subtle in character.

definitions.definition: (of a formula) Having a free variable.

(of a walk) Whose first and last vertices are different.
(of a file, document, etc.) In current use; mapped to part of memory.

definitions.definition: Not fulfilled.

definitions.definition: Not settled or adjusted; not decided or determined; not closed or withdrawn from consideration.

Not of a quality to prevent communication, as by closing waterways, blocking roads, etc.; hence, not frosty or inclement; mild; used of the weather or the climate.

definitions.definition: (Of correspondence) Written or sent with the intention that it may made public or referred to at any trial, rather than by way of confidential private negotiation for a settlement. (Opposite of "without prejudice")

definitions.definition: Uttered with a relatively wide opening of the articulating organs; said of vowels.

(of a syllable) That ends in a vowel; not having a coda.
Made public, usable with a free licence and without proprietary components.
Resulting from an incision, puncture or any other process by which the skin no longer protects an internal part of the body.
A subjective belief, judgment or perspective that a person has formed about a topic, issue, person or thing.
definitions.example: Every man is a fool in some man's opinion.

definitions.definition: The judgment or sentiment which the mind forms of persons or things; estimation.
Favorable estimation; hence, consideration; reputation; fame; public sentiment or esteem.
Obstinacy in holding to one's belief or impression; opiniativeness; conceitedness.

A catamorphism (from the use of banana brackets in the notation).

Curved like a banana, especially of a ball in flight.

Connects at least two alternative words, phrases, clauses, sentences, etc. each of which could make a passage true. In English, this is the "inclusive or." The "exclusive or" is formed by "either [...] or".

A reflexive, often habitual behavior, especially one occurring in a context that often features attempts at deception by persons under psychological stress (such as a poker game or police interrogation), that reveals information that the person exhibiting the behavior is attempting to withhold.
That which is told; a tale or account.
A private message to an individual in a chat room; a whisper.
(archaic outside of idioms) To count, reckon, or enumerate.
definitions.example: All told, there were over a dozen.  Can you tell time on a clock?  He had untold wealth.

definitions.definition: To narrate.
definitions.example: I want to tell a story;  I want to tell you a story.

definitions.definition: To convey by speech; to say.
definitions.example: Finally, someone told him the truth.  He seems to like to tell lies.

definitions.definition: To instruct or inform.
definitions.example: Please tell me how to do it.

definitions.definition: To order; to direct, to say to someone.
definitions.example: Tell him to go away.

definitions.definition: To discern, notice, identify or distinguish.
definitions.example: Can you tell whether those flowers are real or silk, from this distance?  No, there's no way to tell.

definitions.definition: To reveal.
definitions.example: Time will tell what became of him.

definitions.definition: To be revealed.
To have an effect, especially a noticeable one; to be apparent, to be demonstrated.
definitions.example: Sir Gerald was moving slower; his wounds were beginning to tell.

definitions.definition: To use (beads or similar objects) as an aid to prayer.
To inform someone in authority about a wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: (authorship) To reveal information in prose through outright expository statement -- contrasted with show
definitions.example: Maria rewrote the section of her novel that talked about Meg and Sage's friendship to have less telling and more showing.

definitions.definition: A set or group with ten elements.
definitions.example: We divided the chocolates into tens to hand out to Hallowe'en visitors.

definitions.definition: A card in a given suit with a value of ten.
A denomination of currency, such as a banknote, with a value of ten units. See also tenner.

definitions.definition: A perfect specimen, a physically attractive person.
A high level of intensity.
The act of rowing ten strokes flat out.
The number occurring after nine and before eleven, represented in Arabic numerals (base ten) as 10 and in Roman numerals as X.
A sport played by two players (or four in doubles), who alternately strike the ball over a net using racquets.
A match in this sport.
An earlier game in which a ball is driven to and fro, or kept in motion by striking it with a racquet or with the open hand.
To play tennis.
To drive backward and forward like a tennis ball.
Dreadful; causing terror, alarm and fear; awesome

definitions.definition: Formidable, powerful.
Intense; extreme in degree or extent.

definitions.definition: Unpleasant; disagreeable.
definitions.example: The food was terrible, but it was free.

definitions.definition: Very bad; lousy.

definitions.definition: In a terrible way; to a terrible extent; terribly; awfully.
A challenge, trial.
A cupel or cupelling hearth in which precious metals are melted for trial and refinement.
(academia) An examination, given often during the academic term.
A session in which a product or piece of equipment is examined under everyday or extreme conditions to evaluate its durability, etc.
(normally “Test”) A Test match.
The external calciferous shell, or endoskeleton, of an echinoderm, e.g. sand dollars and sea urchins.
Testa; seed coat.
Judgment; distinction; discrimination.
To challenge.

definitions.definition: To refine (gold, silver, etc.) in a test or cupel; to subject to cupellation.
To put to the proof; to prove the truth, genuineness, or quality of by experiment, or by some principle or standard; to try.
definitions.example: to test the soundness of a principle; to test the validity of an argument

definitions.definition: (academics) To administer or assign an examination, often given during the academic term, to (somebody).
To place a product or piece of equipment under everyday and/or extreme conditions and examine it for its durability, etc.
To be shown to be by test.

definitions.definition: To examine or try, as by the use of some reagent.
definitions.example: to test a solution by litmus paper

definitions.definition: A writing consisting of multiple glyphs, characters, symbols or sentences.
A book, tome or other set of writings.
A brief written message transmitted between mobile phones.
Data which can be interpreted as human-readable text.
A verse or passage of Scripture, especially one chosen as the subject of a sermon, or in proof of a doctrine.
(by extension) Anything chosen as the subject of an argument, literary composition, etc.
A style of writing in large characters; also, a kind of type used in printing.

definitions.definition: To send a text message to; i.e. to transmit text using the Short Message Service (SMS), or a similar service, between communications devices, particularly mobile phones.
definitions.example: I'll text the address to you as soon as I find it.

definitions.definition: To send and receive text messages.
definitions.example: Have you been texting all afternoon?

definitions.definition: To write in large characters, as in text hand.
At that time; then.

Introduces a comparison, and is associated with comparatives, and with words such as more, less, and fewer. Typically, it seeks to measure the force of an adjective or similar description between two predicates.
definitions.example: A player than whom none is more skillful.

definitions.definition: Used in comparisons, to introduce the basis of comparison.
definitions.example: she's taller than I am;  she found his advice more witty than helpful;  we have less work today than we had yesterday;  We had no choice than to return home

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside dialectal, usually used with for) Because; for.
An expression of appreciation; a thought.
An entity, usually animate, deemed superior to another; one who has a claim to precedence; a superior.
definitions.example: He quickly found Ali his better in the ring.

definitions.definition: Greater in amount or quantity
(of people)
(of capabilities)
(properties and qualities)
(when with and) Very, extremely. See good and.
definitions.example: The soup is good and hot.

definitions.definition: Holy (especially when capitalized) .
definitions.example: Good Friday

definitions.definition: (of quantities)
In good health.
definitions.example: I had been sick, but now I'm well.

definitions.definition: Good, content.
definitions.example: “How are you?” — “I'm well, thank you!”

definitions.definition: Prudent; good; well-advised.
(manner) Accurately, competently, satisfactorily.
definitions.example: He does his job well.

definitions.definition: (manner) Completely, fully.
definitions.example: We’re well beat now.

definitions.definition: (degree) To a significant degree.
definitions.example: That author is well known.

definitions.definition: (degree) Very (as a general-purpose intensifier).
In a desirable manner; so as one could wish; satisfactorily; favourably; advantageously.
An expression of appreciation; a thought.
To express gratitude or appreciation toward.
definitions.example: She thanked him for the lift.

definitions.definition: To feel gratitude or appreciation toward.
definitions.example: I'll thank you not to smoke in my house!

definitions.definition: To credit or hold responsible.
definitions.example: We can thank global warming for this weather.

definitions.definition: An expression of gratitude.

definitions.definition: Grateful feelings or thoughts.
Used to express appreciation or gratitude.
definitions.example: Could you give me a hand, please? — Yes, sure. — Thanks.

definitions.definition: Something being indicated that is there; one of those.
(degree) To a given extent or degree.

definitions.definition: (degree) To a great extent or degree; very, particularly (in negative constructions).
definitions.example: I did the run last year, and it wasn't that difficult.

definitions.definition: To such an extent; so. (in positive constructions).
definitions.example: Ooh, I was that happy I nearly kissed her.

definitions.definition: (demonstrative) The thing, person, idea, quality, event, action or time indicated or understood from context, especially if more remote geographically, temporally or mentally than one designated as "this", or if expressing distinction.
definitions.example: He went home, and after that I never saw him again.

definitions.definition: The known (thing); used to refer to something just said.
definitions.example: They're getting divorced. What do you think about that?

definitions.definition: (demonstrative) The aforementioned quality or proposition; used to emphatically affirm or deny a previous statement or question.
definitions.example: The water is so cold! — That it is.

definitions.definition: (relative) (plural that) Which, who; representing a subject, direct object, indirect object, or object of a preposition.
definitions.example: The CPR course that she took really came in handy.

definitions.definition: Used in place of relative adverbs such as where or when; often omitted.
definitions.example: the last time that [= when] I went to Europe

definitions.definition: Introducing a clause which is the subject or object of a verb (such as one involving reported speech), or which is a complement to a previous statement.
definitions.example: He told me that the book is a good read.

definitions.definition: Introducing a subordinate clause expressing a reason or cause: because, in that.
definitions.example: Be glad that you have enough to eat.

definitions.definition: Introducing a subordinate clause that expresses an aim, purpose or goal ("final"), and usually contains the auxiliaries may, might or should: so, so that.
Introducing — especially, but not exclusively, with an antecedent like so or such — a subordinate clause expressing a result, consequence or effect.
definitions.example: The noise was so loud that she woke up.

definitions.definition: Introducing a premise or supposition for consideration: seeing as; inasmuch as; given that; as would appear from the fact that.
Introducing a subordinate clause modifying an adverb.
definitions.example: How often did she visit him? — Twice that I saw.

definitions.definition: Introducing an exclamation expressing a desire or wish.
definitions.example: Oh that spring would come!

definitions.definition: Introducing an exclamation expressing a strong emotion such as sadness or surprise.
A place or building, consisting of a stage and seating, in which an audience gathers to watch plays, musical performances, public ceremonies, and so on.
A region where a particular action takes place; a specific field of action, usually with reference to war.

definitions.definition: A lecture theatre.
An operating theatre or locale for human experimentation.

definitions.definition: A cinema.

definitions.definition: Drama or performance as a profession or art form.
definitions.example: I worked in theater for twenty-five years.

definitions.definition: Any place rising by steps like the seats of a theater.
(plural) Those ones.
(singular) Him, her, or it; that one.
Used in comparisons, to introduce the basis of comparison.
definitions.example: she's taller than I am;  she found his advice more witty than helpful;  we have less work today than we had yesterday;  We had no choice than to return home

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside dialectal, usually used with for) Because; for.
That time

definitions.definition: Being so at that time.
(temporal location) At that time.

definitions.definition: (temporal location) Soon afterward.

definitions.definition: (sequence) Next in order; in addition.

definitions.definition: In that case.

definitions.definition: (sequence) At the same time; on the other hand.

definitions.definition: (affirmation) Used to contradict an assertion.
That place.
That status; that position.

definitions.definition: (location) In a place or location (stated, implied or otherwise indicated) at some distance from the speaker (compare here).
In that matter, relation, etc.; at that point, stage, etc., regarded as a distinct place.

definitions.definition: (location) To or into that place; thither.
Where, there where, in which place.
In existence or in this world; see pronoun section below.
Used as an expletive subject of be in its sense of “exist”, with the semantic, usually indefinite subject being postponed or (occasionally) implied.

definitions.definition: Used with other intransitive verbs of existence, in the same sense, or with other intransitive verbs, adding a sense of existence.
definitions.example: If x is a positive number, then there exists [=there is] a positive number y less than x.

definitions.definition: Used with other verbs, when raised.
definitions.example: I expected there to be a simpler solution. [=I expected that there would be a simpler solution.]

definitions.definition: (in combination with certain prepositions, no longer productive) That.

definitions.definition: Used to replace an unknown name, principally in greetings and farewells

definitions.definition: Used to offer encouragement or sympathy.

definitions.definition: Used to express victory or completion.

definitions.definition: (the third-person plural) A group of people, animals, plants or objects previously mentioned.

definitions.definition: (the third-person singular, sometimes proscribed) A single person, previously mentioned, especially if of unknown or non-binary gender, but typically not if previously named and identified as male or female.

definitions.definition: (indefinite pronoun, vague meaning) People; some people; people in general; someone, excluding the speaker.

definitions.definition: That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.
A word, symbol, sign, or other referent that can be used to refer to any entity.
An individual object or distinct entity.
A genuine concept, entity or phenomenon; something that actually exists (often contrary to expectation or belief).

definitions.definition: Whatever can be owned.
Corporeal object.
(somewhat obsolete, with the) The latest fad or fashion.
(in the plural) Clothes, possessions or equipment.

definitions.definition: A unit or container, usually containing edible goods.

definitions.definition: A problem, dilemma, or complicating factor.

definitions.definition: A penis.
A living being or creature.

definitions.definition: That which matters; the crux.

definitions.definition: Used after a noun to refer dismissively to the situation surrounding the noun's referent.

definitions.definition: That which is favoured; personal preference. (Used in possessive constructions.)
A public assembly or judicial council in a Germanic country.
A romantic relationship.
To express as a thing; to reify.
A kind of needle, shorter than a sharp, with a small rounded eye, used for making fine stitches on heavy fabrics.
In the position or interval that separates (two things), or intermediate in quantity or degree. (See Usage notes below.)

definitions.definition: Done together or reciprocally.

definitions.definition: In transit from (one to the other, or connecting places).

definitions.definition: One of (representing a choice).

definitions.definition: An act of thinking; consideration (of something).

definitions.definition: To guess; to reckon.

A third-class degree, awarded to the lowest achievers in an honours degree programme
One sixtieth of a second, i.e., the third in a series of fractional parts in a sexagesimal number system. Also formerly known as a tierce.
To agree with a proposition or statement after it has already been seconded.
To divide into three equal parts.
The ordinal form of the cardinal number three; Coming after the second.

definitions.definition: Needing to drink.

definitions.definition: Causing thirst; giving one a need to drink (informal).
Craving something.

definitions.definition: Desiring sex.

definitions.definition: A rack of thirty beers.
The cardinal number occurring after twenty-nine and before thirty-one, represented in Arabic numerals as 30.
Something being indicated that is here; one of these.
Referring to the current context in a programming environment in C-like languages.
To the degree or extent indicated.

definitions.definition: The thing, item, etc. being indicated.

A numerical value equal to 1,000 = 10 × 100 = 103

definitions.definition: The digit/figure 3.
Anything measuring three units, as length.

definitions.definition: The playing card featuring three pips.
A numerical value after two and before four. Represented in Arabic digits as 3; this many dots (•••).
Describing a set or group with three elements.
A goal or other means of scoring worth three points.
A three-point shot; a basket made from beyond the three-point line.
A three-point landing; an airplane landing in which all three wheels touch the ground simultaneously.
A deer or other animal whose antlers feature three points or tines.
A large slab of stone laid in a dry-stone wall from one side to the other; a perpend.
Passing from one side of something to the other.

definitions.definition: From one end to the other.

definitions.definition: To the end.

definitions.definition: Out into the open.

definitions.definition: From one side of an opening to the other.

definitions.definition: Surrounded by (while moving).

definitions.definition: By means of.

definitions.definition: In consequence of; as a result of.
To (or up to) and including, with all intermediate values.

The best possible hand in lowball.
A motorbike.
(usually in compounds specifying a context) a slut; a promiscuous woman
To travel or exercise using a bicycle.
The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.

definitions.definition: A duration of time.
An instant of time.
The measurement under some system of region of day or moment.

definitions.definition: Ratio of comparison.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Tense.

definitions.definition: The measured duration of sounds; measure; tempo; rate of movement; rhythmical division.

definitions.definition: To measure or record the time, duration, or rate of.

definitions.definition: To choose when something begins or how long it lasts.

definitions.definition: To keep or beat time; to proceed or move in time.
To pass time; to delay.
To regulate as to time; to accompany, or agree with, in time of movement.
To measure, as in music or harmony.
Reminder by the umpire for the players to continue playing after their pause.
The umpire's call in prizefights, etc.
A call by a bartender to warn patrons that the establishment is closing and no more drinks will be served.
To become sleepy or weary.
To make sleepy or weary.
To become bored or impatient (with).

definitions.definition: To bore.
To dress or adorn.
To seize, pull, and tear prey, as a hawk does.
To seize, rend, or tear something as prey; to be fixed upon, or engaged with, anything.
In need of some rest or sleep.
Fed up, annoyed, irritated, sick of.

definitions.definition: Overused, cliché.

definitions.definition: Ineffectual; incompetent
A prefix (honorific) or suffix (post-nominal) added to a person's name to signify either veneration, official position or a professional or academic qualification. See also
Legal right to ownership of a property; a deed or other certificate proving this.

A church to which a priest was ordained, and where he was to reside.
The name of a book, film, musical piece, painting, or other work of art.

definitions.definition: A publication.

definitions.definition: A section or division of a subject, as of a law or a book.

To assign a title to; to entitle.
Toward a closed, touching or engaging position.

definitions.definition: Into the wind.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate the target or recipient of an action.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate result of action.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate a resulting feeling or emotion.

definitions.definition: Used after an adjective to indicate its application.

definitions.definition: Denotes the end of a range.

definitions.definition: As a.

definitions.definition: Coherent; well organized.

definitions.definition: At the same time, in the same place; in close association or proximity.

Foul; dirty.

definitions.definition: A state or condition

(personal) The speakers/writers, or the speaker/writer and at least one other person (not the person being addressed). (This is the exclusive we.)
(personal) The speaker(s)/writer(s) and the person(s) being addressed. (This is the inclusive we.)

(personal) The speaker/writer alone. (This use of we is the editorial we, used by writers and others, including royalty—the royal we—as a less personal substitute for I. The reflexive case of this sense of we is ourself.)

definitions.example: Don't worry, this fabric will wear. These pants will last you for years.  This color wears so well. I must have washed this sweater a thousand times.  I have to say, our friendship has worn pretty well.  It's hard to get to know him, but he wears well.

definitions.example: He hit the target dead in the centre.

definitions.definition: (degree) Very, absolutely, extremely.
definitions.example: dead wrong; dead set; dead serious; dead drunk; dead broke; dead earnest; dead certain; dead slow; dead sure; dead simple; dead honest; dead accurate; dead easy; dead scared; dead solid; dead black; dead white; dead empty

definitions.definition: Suddenly and completely.
definitions.example: He stopped dead.

definitions.definition: As if dead.
definitions.example: dead tired; dead quiet; dead asleep; dead pale; dead cold; dead still

definitions.definition: A division, a portion, a share.
definitions.example: We gave three deals of grain in tribute to the king.

definitions.definition: (often followed by of) An indefinite quantity or amount; a lot (now usually qualified by great or good).
The cessation of life and all associated processes; the end of an organism's existence as an entity independent from its environment and its return to an inert, nonliving state.
definitions.example: The death of my grandmother saddened the whole family.

definitions.definition: (often capitalized) The personification of death as a hooded figure with a scythe; the Grim Reaper. The pronoun he is not the only option, but probably the most traditional one, as it matches with the male grammatical gender of Old English dēaþ, also with cognate German der Tod. The fourth apocalyptic rider (Bible, revelations 6:8) is male θᾰ́νᾰτος (thanatos) in Greek. It has the female name Mors in Latin, but is referred to with male forms qui and eum. The following quotes show this rider on a pale horse is his in the English Bible and she in Peter Gabriel's lyrics.
definitions.example: When death walked in, a chill spread through the room.

definitions.definition: (the death) The collapse or end of something.
definitions.example: England scored a goal at the death to even the score at one all.

definitions.definition: Spiritual lifelessness.
The act of deciding.
A choice or judgement.
definitions.example: It is the decision of the court that movies are protected as free speech.

definitions.definition: Firmness of conviction.
definitions.example: After agonizing deliberations, they finally proceeded with decision.

definitions.definition: (chiefly combat sports) A result arrived at by the judges when there is no clear winner at the end of the contest.
definitions.example: He has won twice by knockout, once by decision.

definitions.definition: A win or a loss awarded to a pitcher.
To defeat an opponent by a decision of the judges, rather than by a knockout
(with "the") The deep part of a lake, sea, etc.
definitions.example: creatures of the deep

definitions.definition: (with "the") A silent time; quiet isolation.
definitions.example: the deep of night

definitions.definition: A deep shade of colour.
The profound part of a problem.
(with "the") The sea, the ocean.
A fielding position near the boundary.
definitions.example: Russell is a safe pair of hands in the deep.

definitions.definition: (of a physical distance) Extending far away from a point of reference, especially downwards.
(intellectual, social) Complex, involved.
(sound, voice) Low in pitch.
definitions.example: She has a very deep contralto voice.

definitions.definition: (of a color) Highly saturated.
definitions.example: That's a very deep shade of blue.

definitions.definition: (sleep) Sound, heavy (describing a state of sleep from which one is not easily awoken).
definitions.example: He was in a deep sleep.

definitions.definition: Immersed, submerged (in).
definitions.example: deep in debt

definitions.definition: Muddy; boggy; sandy; said of roads.
(of time) Distant in the past, ancient.
definitions.example: deep time

definitions.definition: Deeply.
Without question and beyond doubt.
definitions.example: Joe definitely doesn't know how to drive a tractor.

definitions.definition: In a definite manner; decisively.
Good; in acceptable, if not excellent condition.
definitions.example: The car is all right. It gets me there, anyway.

definitions.definition: In good health, unharmed.
definitions.example: I had a headache earlier, but now I'm all right.

definitions.definition: Fairly well.
definitions.example: That went all right, I suppose.

definitions.definition: Most certainly; for sure.
definitions.example: You taught them a lesson all right! They won't be back.

definitions.definition: Used to affirm, indicate agreement, or consent.
definitions.example: All right, let's go then.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate support, favor or encouragement.
definitions.example: All right! They scored!

definitions.definition: Used to fill space or pauses.
definitions.example: All right, so what you suggest we do next?

definitions.definition: Used as a general lead-in or beginning.
definitions.example: All right, let's get started.

definitions.definition: Used to express exasperation or frustration, often with already.
definitions.example: All right, already! Let me finish what I was doing first, and then we can talk.

definitions.definition: Term of greeting, equivalent to how are you or hello.
definitions.example: All right, mate, how are things with you and the missus?

definitions.definition: A stage of proficiency or qualification in a course of study, now especially an award bestowed by a university or, in some countries, a college, as a certification of academic achievement. (In the United States, can include secondary schools.)
definitions.example: She has two bachelor's degrees and is studying towards a master's degree.

definitions.definition: A unit of measurement of angle equal to 1/360 of a circle's circumference.
definitions.example: A right angle is a ninety-degree angle.

definitions.definition: A unit of measurement of temperature on any of several scales, such as Celsius or Fahrenheit.
definitions.example: 180 degrees Fahrenheit is equivalent to 100 degrees Celsius.

definitions.definition: The sum of the exponents of a term; the order of a polynomial.
definitions.example: A quadratic polynomial is a polynomial of degree 2.

definitions.definition: The dimensionality of a field extension.
definitions.example: The Galois field \operatorname{GF}(125) = \operatorname{GF}(5^3) has degree 3 over its subfield \operatorname{GF}(5).

definitions.definition: The number of edges that a vertex takes part in; a valency.
The number of logical connectives in a formula.
The curvature of a circular arc, expressed as the angle subtended by a fixed length of arc or chord.
A unit of measurement of latitude and longitude which together identify a location on the Earth's surface.
(grammar) Any of the three stages (positive, comparative, superlative) in the comparison of an adjective or an adverb.
(obsolete outside heraldry) A step on a set of stairs; the rung of a ladder.
An individual step, or stage, in any process or scale of values.
A stage of rank or privilege; social standing.
(genealogy) A ‘step’ in genealogical descent.
One's relative state or experience; way, manner.
The amount that an entity possesses a certain property; relative intensity, extent.
definitions.example: To what degree do the two accounts of the accident concur?

definitions.definition: A medical doctor who specializes in dentistry.
A part, portion, or subdivision.
A distinct course of life, action, study, or the like.
definitions.example: Technical things are not his department; he's a people person.

definitions.definition: A subdivision of an organization.

A territorial division; a district; especially, in France, one of the districts into which the country is divided for governmental purposes, similar to a county in the UK and in the USA. France is composed of 101 départements organized in 18 régions, each department is divided into arrondissements, in turn divided into cantons.
A military subdivision of a country
definitions.example: the Department of the Potomac

definitions.definition: Act of departing; departure.
(followed by on or upon, formerly also by of) To be contingent or conditioned; to have something as a necessary condition; to hinge on.
definitions.example: We would like to go skiing, but it depends on the amount of snow.

definitions.definition: (followed by on or upon) To trust; to have confidence; to rely.
definitions.example: we depend on the mailman to come at the usual time.

definitions.definition: To hang down; to be sustained by being fastened or attached to something above.
To be pending; to be undetermined or undecided.
definitions.example: a cause depending in court

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) That which is deserved or merited; a just punishment or reward
A person who designs something, or who designs things as a profession.
A plotter or schemer.
A software tool for designing things.
definitions.example: a font designer

definitions.definition: Created by a designer, especially a fashion designer.
definitions.example: a designer briefcase

definitions.definition: A sweet confection served as the last course of a meal
To damage beyond use or repair.
definitions.example: Hooligans destroy unprovoked.

definitions.definition: To neutralize, undo a property or condition.
definitions.example: Smoking destroys the natural subtlety of the palate.

definitions.definition: To put down or euthanize.
definitions.example: Destroying a rabid dog is required by law.

definitions.definition: To severely disrupt the well-being of (a person); ruin.
definitions.example: Her divorce destroyed her; she had a nervous breakdown and was severely depressed for more than a year.

definitions.definition: To defeat soundly.
To remove data.
definitions.example: The memory leak happened because we forgot to destroy the temporary lists.

definitions.definition: To sing a song poorly.
(antiphrasis) To exhaust duly and thus recreate or build up.
definitions.example: This exercise is going to destroy all parts of your shoulders.

definitions.definition: To penetrate sexually in an aggressive way.
definitions.example: She got destroyed by her lover on the carpet.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) A police officer who looks for evidence as part of solving a crime; an investigator.
A person employed to find information not otherwise available to the public.
Employed in detecting.
To change with a specific direction, progress.
definitions.example: Let's see how things develop and then make our decision.

definitions.definition: To progress through a sequence of stages.
definitions.example: Isabel developed from a tropical depression to a tropical storm to a hurricane.   An embryo develops into a fetus and then into an infant.

definitions.definition: To advance; to further; to promote the growth of.
To create.
definitions.example: I need to develop a plan for the next three weeks.

definitions.definition: To bring out images latent in photographic film.
definitions.example: Please develop this roll of film.

definitions.definition: To acquire something usually over a period of time.
definitions.example: I have been in England enough to develop a British accent.

definitions.definition: To place one's pieces actively.
definitions.example: I need to develop my white-square bishop.

definitions.definition: To cause a ball to become more open and available to be played on later. Usually by moving it away from the cushion, or by opening a pack.
To change the form of (an algebraic expression, etc.) by executing certain indicated operations without changing the value.
